document_summary: Introduction of the School of Journalism and Communication at Peking University, co-organizer of the 11th China Communication Conference. Establishes the school as the 'cradle of Journalism and Journalism Education in China' with history dating to 1917. Documents its doctoral programs, national first-class majors, distinguished faculty, and research directions. Serves as source credibility evidence for the conference and supports Liu's institutional affiliation.

snippets.text: [Image: Banner/header image of the School of Journalism and Communication, Peking University website]
snippets.subject: School of Journalism and Communication, Peking University
snippets.reasoning: Website banner image of the School of Journalism and Communication at Peking University, establishing the source of this institutional profile.

snippets.text: Peking University enjoys the honor of being the "cradle of Journalism and Journalism Education in China". In 1917, Prof. Baohuang Xu opened up the optional course of Journalism at Peking University, which marked the start of China's Journalism education. In 1918, Peking University Journalism Research Institute was established, with Yuanpei Cai, the Principal at that time, serving as the President, which further marked the birth of Journalism as a discipline in China. In 2001, the School of Journalism and Communication, Peking University was re- established. Along the way, it gradually formulated a unique research, teaching, and talent- cultivation mode for the discipline of journalism and communication with characteristics of Peking University that fits the era development and addresses international cutting- edge trends.
snippets.subject: School of Journalism and Communication, Peking University
snippets.reasoning: Key passage establishing Peking University as the birthplace of journalism education in China (1917) and journalism as a discipline in China (1918). This historical significance directly supports the credibility of the institution that co-organized the conference where Liu won his award.

snippets.text: This School is authorized to award the doctorate degree of Journalism and Communication, first- level discipline, and to award the doctorate degrees of Communication, Journalism, and New Media, second- level discipline. Additionally, it has established postdoctoral stations for the disciplines of Communication, as well as Journalism. The Journalism of the School of Journalism and Communication was included by the Ministry of Education in the "sixth batch featured majors built in higher education institutions". Journalism and Advertising were included in the national first- class majors, and broadcasting and television were included in the Beijing municipal first- class majors. The Secretariat of National Advisory Committee for Professional Degree in Journalism and Communication Education is set at Peking University.
snippets.subject: School of Journalism and Communication, Peking University
snippets.reasoning: Documents the school's academic authority: doctoral programs, postdoctoral stations, national first-class major designations, and hosting the Secretariat of National Advisory Committee for Journalism and Communication Education. Establishes institutional prestige.

snippets.text: This School has an advanced and sophisticated faculty, including two scholars selected into national key talent schemes, one renowned cultural scholar selected into the "Four Key Principles" Talent Scheme supported by the Publicity Department of CCCPC, one scholar selected into the Program for New Century Excellent Talents in University supported by the Ministry of Education, one leading character in the national broadcasting, television, and network visual- audio industry, and five scholars selected into the "Thousand Talent" Mutual Employment Scheme between Higher Education Institutions and News Entities.
snippets.subject: School of Journalism and Communication, Peking University
snippets.reasoning: Details the distinguished faculty including national-level talent scheme scholars and Ministry of Education selectees, further establishing the elite status of the school that co-organized Liu's award-granting conference.

snippets.text: Scholars of Journalism and Communication add to this discipline and seek ground- breaking perspectives by proposing insightful questions regarding social science research in the digitalized era and producing original research outcomes based on internet communication activities and media forms. Targeted and key research directions mainly include media studies (news, broadcasting, and new media), strategic communication (advertising, public relations, and strategy), and global communication (international communication, cross- cultural communication, public communication, and health communication, etc.). This School has developed featured advantages in the research areas of Jinping Xi's serial keynote speeches about news and public opinions, the Marxist View of Journalism, digitalization and new media, national strategic communication, and advertising development, etc.
snippets.subject: School of Journalism and Communication, Peking University
snippets.reasoning: Describes the school's research directions in media studies, strategic communication, and global communication, which align with Liu's research areas. Establishes the school's focus on cutting-edge digital and new media research.
